folio.label: 40r
folio.l: ¢ Heere bygynneth the Millers tale ;

note.notetxt: L IR The incipit is placed in the header.ODR

folio.l: Whilom there was dwellyng , in Oxenfor¶
A riche gnof , that gestes held to bor¶
And of his craft , he was a Carpentere
W hi~ æäe was dwellyng , a poore scolere
Had lerned art , but al his fantasye
Was turned , for to lerne , Astrologye
And koude a serteyn , of conclusions
To demen , by interrogacions
If æ men asked hi~ , in certeyn houres
What æ men shuld haue , drought or eÂ shoures
Or if men asked hi~ , what shulde by falle
Of euäy thyng , I may nat rekken hem alle
¢ This Clerk was cleped , hende Nicholas
Of derne loue he coude , and of solas
And ther to he was sly , and ful preue
And like a Maide , meke for to se
A Chaumbre had he , in that hostelrye
A lone , with outen ony companye
Ful fetisly digút , with herbes swote
xndAndxAndhe hi~ self , as swete as is the rote20And: The initial A could be a correction from another letter. JT This is possible: double check/ODR/
Of licoris , or ony Setuale
His Almageste , and bokes grete and smale
His Astralabie , longyng for his art
His Augryme stones , loyn faire a pert
On shelues , kouched , at his beddis hede
His presse couäed , with a faldyng rede

note.notetxt: 26 The ink becomes lighter in the following lines.ODR

folio.l: And al a boue , there lay a gay Sautrye
On whiche he mad , on nygútis melodye
So swetely , æat al the chaumber rong
And Angelus ad virginem , he song
And after that , he song the kynges note
Ful often blissed , was his mery throte
And thus this swete Clerk , his tyme spent
After his frendis fyndyng , and his rent´
¢ This Carpenter had wedded , newe a wyf
Whicú æat he loued , more than his lyf
Of xviij ©ere , she was of age
Ielous he was , & held hire narwe in kage
For she was wylde , & ©ong , and he was old
And demed hi~ self , to ben like a Cokewold
He knew nat Catouö , for his wyt was rude
That bad men shulde wedde , here simulitude
Men shulde wedden , after here astate
For ©outhe and age , is often at debate
